ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: Repeated kube:admin pods/exec into adservice running netcat-style probes is high-risk operational access you should confine, justify, and audit like break-glass activity.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Runtime Kubernetes audit evidence shows actual exec with shell and nc against an internal IP; that is exactly what this policy is meant to catch.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Broad pods/exec rights let insiders or stolen credentials pivot through live containers, run network tooling, and harvest secrets mounted in-pod.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: adservice in ms-demo today, but cluster-admin-equivalent actors can reuse the pattern across namespaces if RBAC stays loose.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: HIGH policy severity plus interactive tooling and privileged group membership on the caller beats image-only findings for urgency.
recommended_actions: Remove day-to-day cluster-admin from troubleshooting flows; scope pods/exec to break-glass roles only.
Correlate these execs with change tickets; if legitimate, publish a runbook that avoids nc inside prod pods.
Consider Gatekeeper/OPA guardrails that warn when cluster-admins use exec outside approved maintenance windows.
mitigation_strategies: Enable and ship Kubernetes audit logs for exec/attach to a SIEM rule until RBAC is tightened.
